updates {{!}} Stepanakert under fire as Azerbaijan launches assault on Nagorno-Karabakh |url=https://oc-media.org/live-updates-stepanakert-under-fire-as-war-breaks-out-in-nagorno-karabakh/ |access-date=19 September 2023 |website=OC Media |language=en-US}}{{Cite web |date=19 September 2023 |title=Azerbaijan says it has begun 'anti-terrorist' operations in Nagorno-Karabakh |url=https://www.france24.com/en/asia-pacific/20230919-azerbaijan-says-it-has-begun-anti-terrorist-operations-in-nagorno-karabakh |access-date=19 September 2023 |website=France 24 |language=en}} One day after the offensive started, on 20 September, an agreement on establishing a complete cessation of hostilities in Nagorno-Karabakh was reached at the mediation of the [[Peacekeeping operations in Nagorno-Karabakh|Russian peacekeeping command]] in Nagorno-Karabakh.{{Cite web |title=Russian-mediated ceasefire announced in Nagorno-Karabakh |url=https://armenpress.am/eng/news/1120051.html |website=Armenpress|date=20 September 2023 }}{{cite news |author1=Tim Lister |author2=Anna Chernova |author3=Christian Edwards |author4=Radina Gigova |date=21 September 2023 |title=Azerbaijan says it has retaken breakaway Armenian enclave after separatists surrender |work=CNN.com |url=https://www.cnn.com/2023/09/20/asia/nagorno-karabakh-russia-ceasefire-intl/index.html}} Azerbaijan held a meeting with representatives of the Artsakh Armenian community on 21 September in [[Yevlakh]] and a further meeting took place the following month.{{cite news |date=20 September 2023 |title=Ethnic Armenians accept Russia ceasefire plan after Azerbaijan offensive in Nagorno-Karabakh |language=en |work=[[CNN]] |url=https://edition.cnn.com/2023/09/20/asia/nagorno-karabakh-russia-ceasefire-intl/index.html |url-status=live |access-date=20 September 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230920132915/https://edition.cnn.com/2023/09/20/asia/nagorno-karabakh-russia-ceasefire-intl/index.html |archive-date=20 September 2023}}{{cite news |date=20 September 2023 |title=BREAKING: Stepanakert to disband army in ceasefire deal |work=CIVILNET |url=https://www.civilnet.am/en/news/751112/breaking-stepanakert-to-disband-army-in-ceasefire-deal/ |url-status=live |access-date=20 September 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230920132923/https://www.civilnet.am/en/news/751112/breaking-stepanakert-to-disband-army-in-ceasefire-deal/ |archive-date=20 September 2023}} Ceasefire violations by Azerbaijan were nonetheless reported by both Artsakh and local residents in [[Stepanakert]] on 21 September.{{Cite web |title=МВД Нагорного Карабаха обвинило Азербайджан в нарушении договора о прекращении огня |url=https://meduza.io/news/2023/09/21/mvd-nagornogo-karabaha-obvinilo-azerbaydzhan-v-narushenii-dogovora-o-prekraschenii-ognya |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230921113954/https://meduza.io/news/2023/09/21/mvd-nagornogo-karabaha-obvinilo-azerbaydzhan-v-narushenii-dogovora-o-prekraschenii-ognya |archive-date=21 September 2023 |access-date=21 September 2023 |website=[[Meduza]]}}{{Cite web |date=21 September 2023 |title=Aserbaidschan soll Waffenruhe gebrochen haben |url=https://rp-online.de/politik/ausland/berg-karabach-aserbaidschan-soll-waffenruhe-gebrochen-haben_aid-98086627 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230921114231/https://rp-online.de/politik/ausland/berg-karabach-aliyev-entschuldigt-sich-fuer-tod-russischer-soldaten_aid-98086627 |archive-date=21 September 2023 |access-date=21 September 2023 |website=[[Rheinische Post]]}} === Azerbaijani resettlement === {{main|Azerbaijan's construction in areas gained in the Second Nagorno-Karabakh War}} Following the Azerbaijani reconquest of the region and the [[flight of Nagorno-Karabakh Armenians]], Azerbaijan implemented a program to settle Azerbaijanis in the previously-Armenian-inhabited areas.{{cite web|url=https://caspianpost.com/opinion/the-great-return-azerbaijan-s-post-conflict-revival-model|accessdate=18 September 2025|date=24 March 2025|title=The Great Return: Azerbaijan's Post-Conflict Revival Model}} ==Fatalities== ===1988–1994=== An estimated 28,000–38,000 people were killed between 1988 and 1994.{{#tag:ref|See{{sfn|De Waal|2003|p=285}}}} Armenian military fatalities were reported to be between 5,856{{in lang|ru}} Melik-Shahnazarov, Arsen. [http://www.sumgait.info/caucasus-conflicts/nagorno-karabakh-facts/nagorno-karabakh-facts-14.htm Нагорный Карабах: факты против лжи] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101129070058/http://sumgait.info/caucasus-conflicts/nagorno-karabakh-facts/nagorno-karabakh-facts-14.htm |date=29 November 2010 }}. and 6,000,{{sfn|De Waal|2003|p=285}} while 1,264 Armenian civilians were also killed. Another 196 Armenian soldiers and 400 civilians were missing. According to the Union of Relatives of the Artsakh War Missing in Action Soldiers, as of 2014, 239 Karabakhi soldiers remain officially unaccounted for.{{cite web|url=http://www.stephenmbland.com/#!a-letter-from-stepanakert/c1owq|title=StephenMBland|access-date=26 February 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20161126132658/http://www.stephenmbland.com/#!a-letter-from-stepanakert/c1owq|archive-date=26 November 2016|url-status=live}} Azerbaijan stated 11,557 of its soldiers were killed,{{Cite web |url=http://ru.apa.az/nagornyj_karabakh/nazvano-chislo-azerbajdzhanskikh-voennosluzhashikh-pogibshikh-vo-vremya-i-karabakhskoj-vojny-eksklyuziv.html |title=Названо число азербайджанских военнослужащих, погибших во время I Карабахской войны |access-date=12 May 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180724154005/http://ru.apa.az/nagornyj_karabakh/nazvano-chislo-azerbajdzhanskikh-voennosluzhashikh-pogibshikh-vo-vremya-i-karabakhskoj-vojny-eksklyuziv.html |archive-date=24 July 2018 |url-status=dead }} while Western and Russian estimates of dead combatants on the Azerbaijani side were 25,000–30,000.{{sfn|De Waal|2003|p=285}}[http://www.euronews.net/2009/11/28/winds-of-change-in-nagorno-karabakh/ "Winds of Change in Nagorno Karabakh"]. {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111206040424/http://www.euronews.net/2009/11/28/winds-of-change-in-nagorno-karabakh/ |date=6 December 2011}} ''[[Euronews]]''. 28 November 2009. 4,210 Azerbaijani soldiers{{cite news|last=Ohanyan |first=Karine |author2=Zarema Velikhanova |title=Investigation: Karabakh: Missing in Action – Alive or Dead? |publisher=Institute for War and Peace Reporting |date=12 May 2004 |url=http://iwpr.net/report-news/investigation-karabakh-missing-action-alive-or-dead |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101103044222/http://iwpr.net/report-news/investigation-karabakh-missing-action-alive-or-dead |archive-date=3 November 2010 }} and 749 civilians were also missing. The total number of Azerbaijani civilians killed in the conflict is unknown, although 167–763 were killed on one day in 1992 by the Republic of Nagorno-Karabakh's forces.[[Uppsala Conflict Data Program]], Republic of Nagorno-Karabakh – civilians, viewed 3 May 2013 ===1994–2019=== Although no precise casualty figures exist, between 1994 and 2009, as many as 3,000 people, mostly soldiers, had been killed, according to most observers. In 2008, the fighting became more intense and frequent.{{cite web|url=http://www.ecoi.net/local_link/238240/361172_de.html|title=Armenialiberty:Two Azeri Soldiers Killed In 'Armenian Truce Violation'- ecoi.net – European Country of Origin Information Network|author=Armenialiberty|date=5 February 2013 |access-date=11 April 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150414005358/http://www.ecoi.net/local_link/238240/361172_de.html|archive-date=14 April 2015|url-status=live}} With 72 deaths in 2014, the year became the bloodiest since the war had ended. Two years later, between 1 and 5 April 2016, heavy fighting along the Nagorno-Karabakh front left 91 Armenian (11 non-combat){{Cite web|url=http://asbarez.com/158971/165-armenian-servicemen-died-in-2016/|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200201184941/http://asbarez.com/158971/165-armenian-servicemen-died-in-2016/|url-status=dead|archive-date=1 February 2020|title=165 Armenian Servicemen Died in 2016 | Asbarez.com|date=1 February 2020}} and 94 Azerbaijani soldiers dead, with two missing. In addition, 15 civilians (nine Armenians and six Azerbaijanis) were killed.{{cite web|url=https://armenpress.am/eng/news/842900|title=Serj Tankian calls on supporting families of killed Armenian soldiers|date=9 April 2016 |access-date=9 April 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211204004534/https://armenpress.am/eng/news/842900/|archive-date=4 December 2021|url-status=live}}{{cite web|url=http://www.azernews.az/azerbaijan/94826.html|title=Baku says Armenia's military dictatorship threats values that civilized world stands for|work=AZERNEWS|date=6 April 2016 |access-date=6 April 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160513125719/http://www.azernews.az/azerbaijan/94826.html|archive-date=13 May 2016|url-status=live}} Azerbaijan stated 398 of its soldiers and 31 civilians were killed between 1994 and up to September 2020, right before the start of the 2020 conflict.{{cite web|url=https://news.milli.az/politics/886390.html|title=Atəşkəs dövründən bəri erməni təxribatı nəticəsində həlak olan Azərbaycan hərbçilərinin və mülki şəxslərin sayı açıqlandı|date=3 October 2020 |access-date=3 October 2020}}
[https://tass.com/world/1214559 Baku reports 63 Azeri civilians dead since situation in Nagorno-Karabakh escalated] In comparison, the Caspian Defense Studies Institute [[Non-governmental organization|NGO]] reported 1,008 Azerbaijani soldiers and more than 90 civilians were killed between 1994 and 2016.{{cite web|last1=Tv |first1=Meydan |url=https://www.meydan.tv/en/site/society/14539/|title=Caspian Defense Studies Institute: More than 2000 injured or dead in Karabakh war|work=Meydan TV|access-date=18 May 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160806184714/https://www.meydan.tv/en/site/society/14539/|archive-date=6 August 2016|url-status=live}} {| class="wikitable" |- ! 10+ soldiers |} ===2020–2023=== Between January and September 2020, 16 Azerbaijani1 killed (7 Jan.),[https://www.eng.kavkaz-uzel.eu/articles/49626/] 1 killed (15 Feb.),[https://www.aysor.am/en/news/2020/02/17/azerbaijani-sniper/1660231] 1 killed (5 March),[https://www.aa.com.tr/en/asia-pacific/azerbaijani-soldier-martyred-in-armenian-attack/1756359] 12 killed ( 12–16 July),[https://apnews.com/article/851e96e2177b697c03f020161974c9e8] 1 killed (21 Sep.),[https://www.dailysabah.com/politics/azerbaijani-soldier-killed-as-armenia-violates-cease-fire-in-tovuz/news] total of 16 reported killed and 8 Armenian soldiers,1 killed (10 March),[https://armenpress.am/eng/news/1008058.html] 1 killed (4 April),[https://www.eng.kavkaz-uzel.eu/articles/50797/] 4 killed ( 12–16 July),[https://apnews.com/article/851e96e2177b697c03f020161974c9e8] 1 killed (23 July),[https://mediamax.am/en/news/armypolice/38708/] 1 killed (27 July),[https://en.armradio.am/2020/07/27/armenian-contract-serviceman-killed-in-azerbaijani-sniper-fire/] total of 8 reported killed as well as an Azerbaijani civilian, were killed in sporadic clashes.{{Cite web|url=https://apnews.com/article/851e96e2177b697c03f020161974c9e8|title=Clashes resume on volatile Armenian-Azerbaijani border|date=16 July 2020|website=AP NEWS}} The [[Second Nagorno-Karabakh War]] occurred between 27 September and 10 November 2020. According to Azerbaijan, the fighting left 2,906 Azerbaijani soldiersBy 21 October 2021, the [[Ministry of Defence (Azerbaijan)|Ministry of Defence of the Republic of Azerbaijan]] (MN) published a list of 2,908 servicemen who were confirmed killed during the war,[https://mod.gov.az/en/news/list-of-the-servicemen-fallen-shehids-in-the-patriotic-war-38076.html] although at least two of the soldiers named were killed after the conflict ended,[https://www.azernews.az/karabakh/173267.html][https://sozcu.az/azerbaycan-esgeri-susa-yolunda-minaya-duserek-sehid-oldu/] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220408130119/https://sozcu.az/azerbaycan-esgeri-susa-yolunda-minaya-duserek-sehid-oldu/|date=8 April 2022}} leaving a total of 2,906 servicemen confirmed killed in the war. and 100 civilians dead,{{cite web |url=https://www.azernews.az/aggression/173628.html |title=Civilian death toll in Armenian attacks reaches 100 |author=Ayya Lmahamad |date=8 December 2020 |website=azernews.az }} while six servicemen were still missing.{{Cite web|url=https://mod.gov.az/en/news/list-of-the-servicemen-fallen-shehids-in-the-patriotic-war-38076.html|title=List of the servicemen fallen Shehids in the Patriotic War|website=MINISTRY OF DEFENSE OF THE REPUBLIC OF AZERBAIJAN}} Armenian authorities stated the fighting had left 3,825 Armenian soldiers and 85 civilians dead,By 27 September 2021, 84 civilians were confirmed killed in the conflict, 80 of which died in the Republic of Artsakh and 4 were killed in Armenia.[https://news.am/eng/news/665057.html][https://armenpress.am/eng/news/1031392.html] Another 22 were still missing.[https://www.panarmenian.net/eng/news/295734/] Subsequently, the number of civilians missing was updated to 21 by 21 March 2022,[https://www.armenpress.am/eng/news/1078446/] bringing the total number of confirmed civilian fatalities to 85. while 187 servicemen and 21 civilians were still missing. Additionally, the Syrian Observatory for Human Rights documented the deaths of 541 Syrian mercenaries fighting for Azerbaijan. Two Russian soldiers were also killed when their helicopter was shot down by Azerbaijan by accident while flying in Armenian airspace near the border.{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/news/world-europe-54874162|title=Azerbaijan admits shooting down Russian helicopter in Armenia|work=BBC News|date=9 November 2020}} In addition, a 13-year-old Russian citizen was killed during an Armenian missile strike on the city of Ganja.{{cite news |title=Russian teenager dies in missile attack on Ganja |url=https://news.ru/en/cis-countries/russian-citizen-dies-in-missile-attack-on-ganja/ |access-date=17 November 2020 |work=[[NEWSru]] |date=24 October 2020 |language=en}} In the two-month period fighting, thousands were killed, primarily soldiers, but also an estimated two hundred civilians.{{Cite news |date=20 October 2020 |title=Washington to host talks on Nagorno-Karabakh, warring sides say |url=https://www.reuters.com/article/us-armenia-azerbaijan/nagorno-karabakh-ceasefire-under-severe-strain-after-new-fighting-idUSKBN27510X |access-date=20 October 2020 |newspaper=Reuters}} Following the end of the war, eleven more Azerbaijani soldiers, six Azerbaijani civilians and one Russian peacekeeper were killed in clashes and landmine explosions in the region by the end of 2020.{{Cite web|url=https://en.azvision.az/news/137035/azerbaijan-discloses-number-of-people-killed-in-mine-explosions.html|title=Azerbaijan discloses number of people killed in mine explosions|date=22 December 2020|website=Azvision}}{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/azeri/azerbaijan-55285924|title=Azərbaycan MN: "4 hərbi qulluqçumuz öldürülüb"|newspaper=BBC News Azərbaycanca}}{{Cite news|url=https://www.reuters.com/article/armenia-azerbaijan-int-idUSKBN2920GE|title=Azerbaijan says one serviceman killed in attack in Karabakh|author=|date=28 December 2020|newspaper=Reuters}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.almasdarnews.com/article/first-russian-soldier-killed-in-karabakh-mission/|title=First Russian soldier killed in Karabakh mission|date=19 December 2020|access-date=19 December 2020|archive-date=24 September 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210924205505/https://www.almasdarnews.com/article/first-russian-soldier-killed-in-karabakh-mission/|url-status=dead}} Twelve Azerbaijani civilians18 civilians were killed by landmines between November 2020, and July 2021,[https://english.alarabiya.net/News/world/2021/07/03/Azerbaijan-releases-15-captured-Armenian-soldiers] of which six died in 2020,[https://en.azvision.az/news/137035/azerbaijan-discloses-number-of-people-killed-in-mine-explosions.html] leaving a total of 12 reported killed in 2021. and two soldiers were killed in 2021 by landmine explosions.{{Cite web |url=https://www.aa.com.tr/en/azerbaijan-front-line/azerbaijani-civilian-killed-by-armenian-landmine-blast/2095881 |title=Azerbaijani civilian killed by Armenian landmine blast |website=www.aa.com.tr}}{{Cite web |url=https://menafn.com/1101549090/Azerbaijani-soldier-killed-during-explosion-in-Aghdam-district |title=Azerbaijani soldier killed during explosion in Aghdam district |website=menafn.com}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.azernews.az/karabakh/178069.html|title=Azerbaijani serviceman killed in mine explosion in liberated settlement|date=16 April 2021|website=AzerNews.az}} Seventeen Armenian{{Cite web|url=https://hetq.am/en/article/140696|title=2021: 76 Soldier Deaths in Armenia, Artsakh|website=Hetq.am|date=2 February 2022 }} and ten Azerbaijani soldiers were also killed in shoot-outs in the border area,{{Cite web|url=https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2021/5/25/azerbaijan-denies-firing-at-armenian-positions|title=Armenia says soldier killed in border shoot-out with Azeri forces|website=www.aljazeera.com}}{{Cite news|url=https://www.reuters.com/world/armenian-soldier-killed-by-azeri-sniper-fire-border-tass-2021-09-01/|title=Armenian soldier killed by Azeri sniper fire on border – TASS|newspaper=Reuters |date=1 September 2021|via=www.reuters.com}}{{Cite news|url=https://www.rferl.org/a/armenia-azerbaijan-border-clashes-deaths/31569486.html|title=Armenia Says Six Of Its Soldiers Killed In Latest Clashes With Azerbaijan|newspaper=Radio Free Europe/Radio Liberty|date=19 November 2021 }}{{Cite web|url=https://apnews.com/article/europe-russia-azerbaijan-armenia-075ddbe074af6033e6c3ebcca5dfc00a|title=Armenia, Azerbaijan trade blame for border clashes|date=9 December 2021|website=AP NEWS}} while 38 Armenian soldiers were captured.{{Cite news|url=https://www.azatutyun.am/a/31277024.html|title=Yerevan Wants International Observers Deployed on Armenian-Azeri Border|newspaper=«Ազատ Եվրոպա/Ազատություն» Ռադիոկայան |date=27 May 2021 |access-date=27 May 2021|archive-date=27 May web|url=https://armenianweekly.com/2021/12/01/armenia-and-azerbaijan-agree-to-create-commission-to-demarcate-border/|title=Armenia and Azerbaijan agree to create commission to demarcate web|url=https://news.yahoo.com/azerbaijan-hands-over-10-more-141308516.html|title=Azerbaijan hands over 10 more captured soldiers to Armenia|website=news.yahoo.com|date=19 December 2021 }}{{Cite news|url=https://www.rferl.org/a/azerbaijan-armenia-servicemen-captured-released/31691188.html|title=Azerbaijan Returns Eight Captured Armenian Soldiers|website=RadioFreeEurope/RadioLiberty}} In 2022, three Armenian soldiers were killed and 14 wounded in an attack by Azerbaijani drones in Nagorno-Karabakh on 25 March.{{Cite web |url=https://abcnews.go.com/International/wireStory/nagorno-karabakh-soldiers-killed-drone-attack-83677193 |title=Nagorno-Karabakh says 3 soldiers killed in drone attack |website=ABC News}} During the [[2023 Azerbaijani offensive in Nagorno-Karabakh]], over 190 Azerbaijani and 190 Artsakh soldiers were killed.{{cite web |last=Roth |first=Andrew |date=19 September 2023 |title=Azerbaijan launches 'anti-terrorist' campaign in disputed Nagorno-Karabakh region |url=https://www.theguardian.com/world/2023/sep/19/azerbaijan-launches-anti-terrorist-campaign-in-disputed-nagorno-karabakh-region |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230919111008/https://www.theguardian.com/world/2023/sep/19/azerbaijan-launches-anti-terrorist-campaign-in-disputed-nagorno-karabakh-region |archive-date=19 September 2023 |access-date=19 September 2023 |work=[[The Guardian]]}}{{cite web |date=28 September 2023 |title=Karabakh separatists to disband after surrender to Azerbaijan |url=https://www.france24.com/en/live-news/20230928-nagorno-karabakh-to-dissolve-ending-independence-dream |url-status=live |archive-url=http://web.archive.org/web/20250626103009/https://www.france24.com/en/live-news/20230928-nagorno-karabakh-to-dissolve-ending-independence-dream |archive-date=26 Jun 2025 |access-date=18 December 2023 |website=France 24 |language=en}} 5 Russian peacekeepers were also killed.{{cite web |date=22 September 2023 |title=Russia says Armenian separatists surrender arms after Azerbaijan reclaims Nagorno-Karabakh |url=https://apnews.com/article/azerbaijan-armenia-nagorno-karabakh-5488386cb18c4333d00721365730cdf9 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://archive.today/20230927212915/https://apnews.com/article/azerbaijan-armenia-nagorno-karabakh-5488386cb18c4333d00721365730cdf9 |archive-date=27 September 2023 |access-date=18 December 2023 |website=AP News |language=en}}{{cite web |date=20 September 2023 |title=Russian-mediated ceasefire announced in Nagorno-Karabakh |url=https://armenpress.am/eng/news/1120051.html |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230921131534/https://armenpress.am/eng/news/1120051.html |archive-date=21 September 2023 |access-date=20 September 2023 |work=Armenpress}} Subsequently, over 100,000 ethnic Armenian inhabitants of Nagorno-Karabakh [[Flight of Nagorno-Karabakh Armenians|fled the region]].{{Cite news |last=Ռ/Կ |first=«Ազատություն» |date=15 November 2023 |title=U.S. Says Nagorno-Karabakh Armenians Entitled To Return Home |url=https://www.azatutyun.am/a/32685339.html |access-date=10 February 2024 |work=«Ազատ Եվրոպա/Ազատություն» ռադիոկայան |language=hy}} ==Foreign involvement== ===Countries=== ====Russia==== [[File:С Президентом Азербайджана Ильхамом Алиевым и Президентом Армении Сержем Саргсяном - 2.jpeg|thumb|Azerbaijani President [[Ilham Aliyev]], Russian President [[Dmitry Medvedev]] and Armenian President [[Serzh Sargsyan]] on 23 January 2012]] Russia is officially neutral and has sought to play the role of a mediator.{{cite news |title=Russia styles itself lead mediator in Nagorno-Karabakh conflict |url=https://br.reuters.com/article/us-nagorno-karabakh-idUSKCN0X41Y6 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201007045614/https://br.reuters.com/article/us-nagorno-karabakh-idUSKCN0X41Y6 |url-status=dead |archive-date=7 October 2020 |work=Reuters |date=7 April 2016}}{{cite news |title=Armenia-Azerbaijan conflict: Why Caucasus flare-up risks wider war |url=https://www.bbc.com/news/world-europe-54356336 |agency=BBC News |date=2 October 2020}}{{cite news |title=Turkey's support role complicates latest Nagorno-Karabakh flare-up |url=https://www.ft.com/content/f9fc56fb-e4c4-4fb8-a743-bcdb1d862d2c |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201001201922/https://www.ft.com/content/f9fc56fb-e4c4-4fb8-a743-bcdb1d862d2c |archive-date=1 October 2020 |url-access=subscription |url-status=bot: unknown |work=Financial Times |date=29 September 2020 |access-date=2 October 2020 }} In its official statements, Russia calls for a peaceful settlement and restraint during skirmishes.{{cite news |title=Putin, Macron call for Nagorno-Karabakh ceasefire as deaths mount |url=https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2020/10/1/putin-macron-call-for-nagorno-karabakh-ceasefire-as-deaths-mount |publisher=Al Jazeera |date=1 October 2020}} British journalist Thomas de Waal has argued that there is an Azerbaijani narrative that Russia has "consistently supported the Armenian side." According to de Waal, Russia "has more supported the Armenian side," but there have been various "different Russian actors at different times supporting both sides in this conflict." He argues that President [[Boris Yeltsin]] did not "want to see the Armenian side be defeated, but he also didn't want to supply them with too many weapons." De Waal concluded in 2012 that "Russia [is] playing both sides", but "ultimately more in the Armenian side."{{cite web |last1=Harmala |first1=Jason |last2=Wilson |first2=Ross |title=Transcript: Russia's Aims and Priorities in Nagorno-Karabakh |url=https://www.atlanticcouncil.org/commentary/transcript/russias-aims-and-priorities-in-nagornokarabakh-10-1-12-transcript/ |website=atlanticcouncil.org |publisher=[[Atlantic Council]] |archive-url=https://archive.today/20200809201010/https://www.atlanticcouncil.org/commentary/transcript/russias-aims-and-priorities-in-nagornokarabakh-10-1-12-transcript/ |archive-date=9 August 2020 |date=1 October 2012}} Other commentators have argued that Russia plays both sides in the conflict.Interview with [[Gerard Toal]]. {{cite web |last1=Sanamyan |first1=Emil |title=Q&A with Gerard Toal on Russia's Foreign Interventions |url=https://armenian.usc.edu/qa-with-gerard-toal-on-russias-foreign-interventions/ |website=armenian.usc.edu |publisher=[[USC Institute of Armenian Studies]] |archive-date=9 August 2020 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20200809200527/https://armenian.usc.edu/qa-with-gerard-toal-on-russias-foreign-interventions/ |date=26 June 2017 |quote=People reasonably argue that Russia is playing both sides...}}{{cite web |last1=Larsen |first1=Joseph |title=Russia is Stacking Both Decks in Armenia and Azerbaijan |url=http://gip.ge/russia-is-stacking-both-decks-in-armenia-and-azerbaijan/ |website=gip.ge |publisher=Georgian Institute of Politics |archive-url=https://archive.today/20200804145105/http://gip.ge/russia-is-stacking-both-decks-in-armenia-and-azerbaijan/ |archive-date=4 August 2020 |date=1 December 2016 |quote=Russia, perhaps the conflict's most important party, is neutral but very openly playing both sides.}} [[Svante Cornell]] argued in 2018 that Russia "had been playing both sides of the Armenia–Azerbaijan conflict to gain maximum control over both, a policy that continues to this day."{{cite news |last1=Cornell |first1=Svante |author-link1=Svante Cornell |title=Armenia's Crisis of Legitimacy |url=https://www.the-american-interest.com/2018/04/26/armenias-crisis-legitimacy/ |work=[[The American Interest]] |date=26 April 2018 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20200809200722/https://www.the-american-interest.com/2018/04/26/armenias-crisis-legitimacy/ |archive-date=9 August 2020}} During the first Nagorno-Karabakh War, Russia was widely viewed as supporting the Armenian position due to it providing Armenia with military assistance, including arms and indirect logistical support.{{cite book|last=Benson|first=Brett V.|title=Constructing International Security: Alliances, Deterrence, and Moral Hazard|year=2012|publisher=Cambridge University Press|location=Cambridge|isbn=978-1-107-02724-4|page=67}} Russia supplied around $1 billion worth of weapons and, thus, "made a vital contribution to the Armenian victory." According to de Waal, "greater Russian support for the Armenians" was one of the main factors behind the Armenian victory.{{sfn|De Waal|2003|p=206}} De Waal notes, "Yet it is not entirely clear how this support for the Armenians was translated on to the battlefield; to complicate things further, the Russians also gave some assistance to Azerbaijan."{{sfn|De Waal|2003|p=200}} Following the first Nagorno-Karabakh War and up until 2022, Russia was Armenia's main arms supplier and the two countries are military allies.{{cite news |last1=Buniatian |first1=Heghine |title=Armenia, Russia Sign More Arms Deals |url=https://www.azatutyun.am/a/29796641.html |work=azatutyun.am |agency=[[RFE/RL]] |date=28 February 2019 |quote=The military alliance with Russia entitles Armenia to buying Russian weapons at discounted prices. Moscow lent the Armenian government $200 million for such arms acquisitions in 2015.}}{{cite news |last1=Bhutia |first1=Sam |title=Armenia-Azerbaijan: Who's the big defense spender? |url=https://eurasianet.org/armenia-azerbaijan-whos-the-big-defense-spender |work=[[EurasiaNet]] |date=28 October 2019 |quote=Russia, one of the largest arms exporters in the world, sells weapons to both sides in the conflict – though Armenia, as a member of the Russia-led Collective Security Treaty Organization, gets a discount.}} Russia is sometimes described as Armenia's supporter in the conflict,{{cite news |last1=Jones |first1=Dorian |title=Azerbaijan-Armenia Clashes Highlight Turkey-Russia Rift |url=https://www.voanews.com/a/europe_azerbaijan-armenia-clashes-highlight-turkey-russia-rift/6192936.html |agency=[[Voice of America]] |date=16 July 2020 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20200804144448/https://www.voanews.com/europe/azerbaijan-armenia-clashes-highlight-turkey-russia-rift |archive-date=4 August 2020 |url-status=live |quote=The latest clashes indirectly pit Turkey against Russia. Turkey backs Azerbaijan, while Russia supports Armenia.}}{{cite news |last1=Sofuoglu |first1=Murat |title=Why Russia supports Armenia against Azerbaijan in the Caucasus conflict |url=https://www.trtworld.com/magazine/why-russia-supports-armenia-against-azerbaijan-in-the-caucasus-conflict-38321 |agency=[[TRT World]] |date=23 July 2020 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20200804144412/https://www.trtworld.com/magazine/why-russia-supports-armenia-against-azerbaijan-in-the-caucasus-conflict-38321 |archive-date=4 August 2020}} however, this view is widely challenged as Russia extensively sells arms to Azerbaijan.{{cite news |last1=De Waal |first1=Thomas |author-link1=Thomas de Waal |date=17 July 2020 |title=New old dynamics at play in the Armenia-Azerbaijan conflict |publisher=[[Middle East Institute]] |url=https://www.mei.edu/publications/new-old-dynamics-play-armenia-azerbaijan-conflict |archive-url=https://archive.today/20200804143421/https://www.mei.edu/publications/new-old-dynamics-play-armenia-azerbaijan-conflict |archive-date=4 August 2020 |quote=...local factors are still the main driver of the conflict and that Russia has equities on both sides.}}{{cite news |last1=Nemtsova |first1=Anna |date=13 April 2017 |title=In Nagorno-Karabakh, a Bloody New War With Putin on Both Sides |work=[[The Daily Beast]] |url=https://www.thedailybeast.com/in-nagorno-karabakh-a-bloody-new-war-with-putin-on-both-sides |archive-url=https://archive.today/20200804143718/https://www.thedailybeast.com/in-nagorno-karabakh-a-bloody-new-war-with-putin-on-both-sides |archive-date=4 August 2020}}{{cite news |date=9 April 2016 |title=Russia says won't halt arms sales to arch foes Armenia and Azerbaijan |work=Reuters |url=https://www.reuters.com/article/us-nagorno-karabakh-russia-arms/russia-says-wont-halt-arms-sales-to-arch-foes-armenia-and-azerbaijan-idUSKCN0X60DS |archive-url=https://archive.today/20200804145249/https://www.reuters.com/article/us-nagorno-karabakh-russia-arms/russia-says-wont-halt-arms-sales-to-arch-foes-armenia-and-azerbaijan-idUSKCN0X60DS |archive-date=4 August 2020}}{{cite news |last1=Agayev |first1=Zulfugar |date=13 August 2013 |title=Azeri-Russian Arms Trade $4 Billion Amid Tension With Armenia |url=https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2013-08-13/azeri-russian-arms-trade-4-billion-amid-tension-with-armenia |url-status=live |archive-url=https://archive.today/20200827175856/https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2013-08-13/azeri-russian-arms-trade-4-billion-amid-tension-with-armenia |archive-date=27 August 2020 |agency=Bloomberg News}} Also, in February 2022, Aliyev and Putin signed a "Declaration of Allied Interaction" which elevated military ties between their countries and affirms "mutual respect for ... [the] territorial integrity and inviolability of the borders of the two countries" and obliges Azerbaijan and Russia to "refrain from any actions, including those carried out through third states, directed against each other."{{Cite news |last=Ռ/Կ |first=«Ազատություն» |date=22 February 2022 |title=Russia, Azerbaijan Agree On 'Allied' Ties |language=hy |work=«Ազատ Եվրոպա/Ազատություն» ռադիոկայան |url=https://www.azatutyun.am/a/31717311.html |access-date=11 July 2023}}{{Cite web |title=Azerbaijan's 'neutrality' and the Declaration of Allied Interaction with Russia |url=https://oc-media.org/features/azerbaijans-neutrality-and-the-declaration-of-allied-interaction-with-russia/ |access-date=11 July 2023 |website=OC Media |date=14 March 2022 |language=en-US}} The agreement also has provisions for joint military operations and assistance between Russian and Azerbaijan, including personnel training and production, maintenance, repair, and modernization of military equipment.{{Cite news |title=Azerbaijan and Russia Sign Declaration on Allied Cooperation |language=en-US |publisher=Jamestown |url=https://jamestown.org/program/azerbaijan-and-russia-sign-declaration-on-allied-cooperation/ |access-date=21 July 2023}} However, up until 2022 Armenia purchased Russian weaponry at a discount, while Azerbaijan pays the full price.{{cite web |last1=Markedonov |first1=Sergey |title=Russia and the Nagorno-Karabakh Conflict: A Careful Balancing |url=https://www.ispionline.it/it/pubblicazione/russia-and-nagorno-karabakh-conflict-careful-balancing-19832 |website=ispionline.it |publisher=[[Institute for International Political Studies]] |archive-url=https://archive.today/20200814115324/https://www.ispionline.it/it/pubblicazione/russia-and-nagorno-karabakh-conflict-careful-balancing-19832 |archive-date=14 August 2020 |date=18 March 2018}} In 2022, Russia suspended arms deliveries to Armenia.{{Cite news |last=Ivanova |first=Polina |date=15 August 2023 |title='People feel let down by Russia': disputed Caucasus enclave choked by blockade |work=Financial Times |url=https://www.ft.com/content/d5488713-bd05-4895-abc3-e936a42664ba |access-date=15 August 2023}}{{Cite news |last=Rose |first=Armin |date=31 July 2023 |title=It's Bad to Be a Sheep |work=Tablet Magazine |url=https://www.tabletmag.com/sections/israel-middle-east/articles/armenia-and-israel-armin-rosen}} ====Turkey==== [[File:Recep Tayyip Erdogan 2020 visit to Baku with Ilham Aliyev 44.jpg|thumb|Ilham Aliyev and Turkish President [[Recep Tayyip Erdoğan]] on 25 February 2020]] Turkey is widely considered Azerbaijan's main supporter in the conflict.{{cite news |last1=Safarova |first1=Durna |title=Coup Attempt Ripple Effect: Azerbaijan Closes TV Station |url=https://eurasianet.org/coup-attempt-ripple-effect-azerbaijan-closes-tv-station |work=[[EurasiaNet]] |date=19 July 2016 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20200804195952/https://eurasianet.org/coup-attempt-ripple-effect-azerbaijan-closes-tv-station |archive-date=4 August 2020 |quote=Turkey [...] has served as the Caspian-Sea country's chief ally in Baku's nearly 30-year struggle with Armenia for control of the Nagorno-Karabakh enclave.}}{{cite news |last1=McLaughlin |first1=Daniel |title=World powers call for Azeri-Armenian ceasefire |url=https://www.irishtimes.com/news/world/europe/world-powers-call-for-azeri-armenian-ceasefire-1.2596954 |newspaper=[[The Irish Times]] |date=4 April 2016 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20200804200741/https://www.irishtimes.com/news/world/europe/world-powers-call-for-azeri-armenian-ceasefire-1.2596954 |archive-date=4 August 2020 |quote=Turkey, which is Azerbaijan's chief ally...}}{{cite news |title=Ahmet Davutoglu: I'm proud of Azerbaijan's rapid development that I see each time I visit the country |url=https://azertag.az/en/xeber/Ahmet_Davutoglu_Im_proud_of_Azerbaijans_rapid_development_that_I_see_each_time_I_visit_the_country-796798?__cf_chl_jschl_tk__=5af86670590c24584eef1981e5367c36abc81905-1596571068-0-ATCSLA_wjMwSvkRaL3Z36XaMyYhqxAv4eQdIilk1d_mXV1oKZf4LmEBQIHkSUyym-Iq4X_BCUgfViY86tCsuycHCC0aJMpS_Ipqa_bmjLIYj8wUjlQwwqyf1oOBwv9odReq6Xvz1HNQautig013pwHpZbra_bd9biLSC-4FDnflGBhAZ_XdFrihKX_7j001jHybup_YKw79dVstSSPxWYUOiyM0zEjKtgb2qQ2kwJPF_L0DwB7OcS3llGpfEv2e9jL-rPeRK-dzNR7c7gOkKx_7H9bvYM_wrjcXlSNHs8R6EBkMmEI9LEvI7KY-qQ5BPjoX1j2TcJwO1RZLB6YORhZToBc23sq4KpluMFeHrubFETZzSAjJiop2_gmhEvP6LBHQ74uz84ZzhQswBmyH3ziTvwBEFc7luoENsJQhu-E_Q |work=azertag.az |agency=[[Azerbaijan State News Agency]] |date=19 September 2014 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20200804200229/https://azertag.az/en/xeber/Ahmet_Davutoglu_Im_proud_of_Azerbaijans_rapid_development_that_I_see_each_time_I_visit_the_country-796798?__cf_chl_jschl_tk__=293ce1bede71ef0ef0ccb3adb76a5108ab96db1e-1596571349-0-Adh0lh_9-NjvMym_wqyarLcIoycoO16w4BdZHdIiGEprMc_Fq-5NrHMC69dSBbB5xVpbv11hrrm_2lFHEIZGqgZCYvHXwha_q0sZ9MdpXXAu-n3JAQgyFgSVI_HYxglMqQYMruKeXFen8SEwqIvAUjBk7TXFly_OhdbLS9eJXENlv9g6pUAWTd11g0PotpftH92zckuPdZckH4AN7PgY9gZ_tyAySdYrzVmMP_KJQUCLUA7HcMqLf9ev9EJ03byzYXreJblJhwDPotCP6RoTdOcUeEM2SwoBGFm54HWN7VNflX4Si0_q1jrZXg1aRpz0LFDhmd3RrW4zCQ-TYcEGiHXlTNHDiJukMPfvyO-aemCSXFwHxFb7rJq_p5_WNb5mnGkijej1pnQGnFLLT8HaI7Itg_hVnmrNKqVpIFtBCsy0 |archive-date=4 August 2020 |quote=On the Armenia-Azerbaijan Nagorno-Karabakh conflict, he said “Turkey is our main supporter on this issue”.}} Svante Cornell wrote in 1998 that Turkey is the "only country that constantly expressed its support for Azerbaijan."{{sfn|Cornell|1998|p=51}} It provided Azerbaijan "active military help" during the war.{{cite book|last=Balayev|first=Bahruz|title=The Right to Self-Determination in the South Caucasus: Nagorno Karabakh in Context|year=2013|publisher=Lexington Books|isbn=978-0739178287|page=70 }} Turkey also supports Azerbaijan diplomatically.{{cite news |title=Nagorno-Karabakh clash: Turkey backs Azeris 'to the end' against Armenia |url=https://www.bbc.com/news/world-europe-35953358 |agency=[[BBC News]] |date=3 April 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200320224101/https://www.bbc.com/news/world-europe-35953358 |archive-date=20 March 2020}}{{cite news |title=Turkey reiterates support to Azerbaijan against Armenia |url=https://www.aa.com.tr/en/politics/turkey-reiterates-support-to-azerbaijan-against-armenia/1919117 |agency=[[Anadolu Agency]] |date=22 July 2020 }} Turkish and Azerbaijani armed forces cooperate extensively{{cite news |last1=Abbasov |first1=Shahin |title=Azerbaijan-Turkey Military Pact Signals Impatience with Minsk Talks – Analysts |url=https://eurasianet.org/azerbaijan-turkey-military-pact-signals-impatience-with-minsk-talks-analysts |agency=[[EurasiaNet]] |date=18 January 2011 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20200805092443/https://eurasianet.org/azerbaijan-turkey-military-pact-signals-impatience-with-minsk-talks-analysts |archive-date=5 August 2020}} and regularly hold [[military exercise]]s.{{cite news |title=Azerbaijan, Turkey start joint military exercises |url=https://www.aa.com.tr/en/world/azerbaijan-turkey-start-joint-military-exercises/913179 |work=aa.com.tr |agency=[[Anadolu Agency]] |date=18 September 2017 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20200805092129/https://www.aa.com.tr/en/world/azerbaijan-turkey-start-joint-military-exercises/913179 |archive-date=5 August 2020}}{{cite news |last1=Elliott |first1=Raffi |title=Armenia 'Vigilant' as Joint Turkish-Azeri Military Exercises Begin |url=https://armenianweekly.com/2020/07/29/armenia-vigilant-as-joint-turkish-azeri-military-exercises-begin/ |work=[[The Armenian Weekly]] |date=29 July 2020 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20200805092128/https://armenianweekly.com/2020/07/29/armenia-vigilant-as-joint-turkish-azeri-military-exercises-begin/ |archive-date=5 August 2020}} Azerbaijan has also bought weapons from Turkey.{{cite news |last1=Oruç |first1=Merve Şebnem |title=What is role of foreign players in Azerbaijan-Armenia conflict? |url=https://www.dailysabah.com/opinion/columns/what-is-role-of-foreign-players-in-azerbaijan-armenia-conflict |work=[[Daily Sabah]] |date=22 July 2020 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20200807183847/https://www.dailysabah.com/opinion/columns/what-is-role-of-foreign-players-in-azerbaijan-armenia-conflict |archive-date=7 August 2020 |quote=Baku has bought arms from Turkey and imported Altay tanks, T129 ATAK helicopters, unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) and armed unmanned aerial vehicles (AUAV).}} Turkey closed its border with Armenia in April 1993 after Armenian forces [[Battle of Kalbajar|captured Kalbajar]].{{cite news |last1=Hakobyan |first1=Tatul |author-link1=Tatul Hakobyan |title=Ankara's Response to Yerevan |url=https://hetq.am/en/article/10111 |work=[[Hetq]] |date=3 April 2006 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20200809143015/https://hetq.am/en/article/10111 |archive-date=9 August 2020}} Prior to that, the border was only open "on demand and only for transferring the humanitarian aid (mainly wheat delivery) to Armenia and for the operation of the weekly Kars-Gyumri train, which had been crossing the Turkish–Armenian border since the days of the Soviet Union."{{cite journal |last1=Grigoryan |first1=Aleksandr |last2=Khachatryan |first2=Knar |last3=Ter-Matevosyan |first3=Vahram |title=Armenia-Turkey border opening: what determines the attitude of Armenians? |journal=Caucasus Survey |date=2019 |volume=7 |issue=1 |pages=3–4 |doi=10.1080/23761199.2018.1499298|s2cid=159017448}} Turkey has repeatedly refused to normalize and establish diplomatic relations with Armenia in solidarity with Azerbaijan over Karabakh.{{cite news |last1=Yackley |first1=Ayla Jean |title=Feted in Baku, Turkey's Erdoğan rules out restoring ties with Armenia |url=https://eurasianet.org/feted-in-baku-turkeys-erdogan-rules-out-restoring-ties-with-armenia |agency=[[EurasiaNet]] |date=17 September 2018 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20200819175657/https://eurasianet.org/feted-in-baku-turkeys-erdogan-rules-out-restoring-ties-with-armenia |archive-date=19 August 2020}}{{cite news |last1=Landler |first1=Mark |last2=Arsu |first2=Sebnem |title=After Hitch, Turkey and Armenia Normalize Ties |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2009/10/11/world/europe/11armenia.html |work=[[The New York Times]] |date=10 October 2009}} Turkey and Azerbaijan signed the "Shusha Declaration" in Shusha, a city that Azerbaijan captured during the Second Nagorno-Karabakh War.{{Cite web |date=23 June 2021 |title=Turkey and Azerbaijan's Shusha Declaration adds to Armenia's isolation |url=https://emerging-europe.com/news/turkey-and-azerbaijans-shusha-declaration-adds-to-armenias-isolation/ |access-date=24 July 2023 |website=Emerging Europe |language=en-GB}}{{Cite web |title=The 'Shusha Declaration': Strategic realignment or business as usual? |url=https://oc-media.org/features/the-shusha-declaration-strategic-realignment-or-business-as-usual/ |access-date=24 July 2023 |website=OC Media |date=24 June 2021 |language=en-US}}{{Cite news |date=16 June 2021 |title=In Karabakh, Turkish and Azerbaijani leaders cement alliance |work=Eurasianet |url=https://eurasianet.org/in-karabakh-turkish-and-azerbaijani-leaders-cement-alliance}} The declaration reaffirmed the military and economic cooperation between the countries and Turkish President Erdogan said the opening of a Turkish consulate in Shusha "would be a message to the world and especially to Armenia."{{Cite web |date=13 June 2023 |title=Turkey says it's ready to open a consulate in a city that Azerbaijan took from Armenian forces |url=https://www.pbs.org/newshour/world/turkey-says-its-ready-to-open-consulate-in-city-that-azerbaijan-took-from-armenian-forces |access-date=24 July 2023 |website=PBS NewsHour |language=en-us}} ====Iran==== [[Iran]] is officially neutral and has sought to play the role of a mediator,{{cite news |title=Ayatollah Khamenei: Iran, Armenia should have solid, amicable ties despite U.S. opposition |url=https://www.tehrantimes.com/news/433513/Ayatollah-Khamenei-Iran-Armenia-should-have-solid-amicable |work=[[Tehran Times]] |date=27 February 2019 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20200814120151/https://www.tehrantimes.com/news/433513/Ayatollah-Khamenei-Iran-Armenia-should-have-solid-amicable |archive-date=14 August 2020 |quote=Ayatollah Khamenei reiterated Iran's long-held position that the Islamic Republic is ready to help resolve the conflict between Armenia and the Republic of Azerbaijan.}} * {{cite news |title=Iran proposes to mediate in Karabakh |url=https://www.tehrantimes.com/news/179283/Iran-proposes-to-mediate-in-Karabakh |work=[[Tehran Times]] |date=6 October 2008 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20200814120145/https://www.tehrantimes.com/news/179283/Iran-proposes-to-mediate-in-Karabakh |archive-date=14 August 2020}} most notably [[Tehran Communiqué|in 1992]]. In its official statements, Iran calls for a peaceful settlement{{cite web |title=Spokesman Urges Peaceful Settlement of Karabakh Dispute |url=https://en.mfa.ir/portal/newsview/579145/spokesman-urges-peaceful-settlement-of-karabakh-dispute |website=en.mfa.ir |publisher=Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Iran |archive-url=https://archive.today/20200809133637/https://en.mfa.ir/portal/newsview/579145/spokesman-urges-peaceful-settlement-of-karabakh-dispute |archive-date=9 August 2020 |date=31 March 2020}} and restraint during skirmishes.{{cite news |title=Iran calls on Azerbaijan, Armenia to exercise self-restraint |url=https://en.irna.ir/news/82020736/Iran-calls-on-Azerbaijan-Armenia-to-exercise-self-restraint |work=en.irna.ir |agency=[[Islamic Republic News Agency]] |date=3 April 2016 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20200809133847/https://en.irna.ir/news/82020736/Iran-calls-on-Azerbaijan-Armenia-to-exercise-self-restraint |archive-date=9 August 2020}} At the same time, Iranian officials have repeatedly reaffirmed their support for Azerbaijan's territorial integrity.{{efn|These include, among others, President [[Mohammad Khatami]] in 2004,{{cite news |title=Iran Report |url=https://www.rferl.org/a/1342695.html |work=rferl.org |agency=[[RFE/RL]] |date=17 August 2004 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20200809121722/https://www.rferl.org/a/1342695.html |archive-date=9 August 2020 |quote=Khatami said Iran is ready to contribute to a peaceful solution of the conflict. He added that Iran considers Nagorno-Karabakh part of Azerbaijan and that the use of force in settling international problems is "unacceptable."}} Chief of Staff of Iran's Armed Forces [[Mohammad Bagheri (general)|Mohammad Bagheri]] in 2019,{{cite news |title=General: Iran-Azerbaijan borders secure |url=https://en.irna.ir/news/83174652/General-Iran-Azerbaijan-borders-secure |work=en.irna.ir |agency=[[Islamic Republic News Agency]] |date=17 January 2019 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20200809121913/https://en.irna.ir/news/83174652/General-Iran-Azerbaijan-borders-secure |archive-date=9 August 2020 |quote=General Baqeri further said Iran decisively supports territorial integrity of Azerbaijan Republic.}} Chief of Staff of the President of Iran [[Mahmoud Vaezi]] in 2020,{{cite news |title=Vaezi: Preserving territorial integrity of countries is Iran's regional strategy |url=https://en.irna.ir/news/83855797/Vaezi-Preserving-territorial-integrity-of-countries-is-Iran-s |work=en.irna.ir |agency=[[Islamic Republic News Agency]] |date=14 July 2020 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20200809121959/https://en.irna.ir/news/83855797/Vaezi-Preserving-territorial-integrity-of-countries-is-Iran-s |archive-date=9 August 2020 |quote=...Vaezi said preserving the territorial integrity of other countries namely Azerbaijan has been Iran's regional strategy.}} and Iran's ambassadors in Azerbaijan.{{cite news |title=Iran's policy to support Azerbaijan Republic territorial integrity 'unchangeable': Envoy |url=https://en.irna.ir/news/82478097/Iran-s-policy-to-support-Azerbaijan-Republic-territorial-integrity |work=en.irna.ir |agency=[[Islamic Republic News Agency]] |date=30 March 2017 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20200809122559/https://en.irna.ir/news/82478097/Iran-s-policy-to-support-Azerbaijan-Republic-territorial-integrity |archive-date=9 August 2020}}{{cite news |title=Iran, Azerbaijan keen on boosting relations |url=https://en.irna.ir/news/83888319/Iran-Azerbaijan-keen-on-boosting-relations |work=en.irna.ir |agency=[[Islamic Republic News Agency]] |archive-url=https://archive.today/20200730065516/https://en.irna.ir/news/83888319/Iran-Azerbaijan-keen-on-boosting-relations |archive-date=30 July 2020 |quote=Reaffirming Iran's support to Azerbaijan Republic's territorial integrity, outgoing ambassador said that concerns of Azerbaijan Republic are also concerns of Iran.}}}} Deputy Foreign Minister [[Abbas Araghchi]] stated in 2020 that "While respecting the territorial integrity of the Azerbaijan Republic, Iran is fundamentally opposed to any move that would fuel conflict between the two neighbouring countries of the Azerbaijan Republic and Armenia."{{cite web |title=Iranian, Azeri Diplomats Hold Phone Talks |url=https://en.mfa.ir/portal/newsview/591823/iranian-azeri-diplomats-hold-phone-talks |website=en.mfa.ir |publisher=Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Iran |archive-url=https://archive.today/20200809133638/https://en.mfa.ir/portal/newsview/591823/iranian-azeri-diplomats-hold-phone-talks |archive-date=9 August 2020 |date=16 April 2020}} During the war, "Iran was domestically torn in devising a policy", but ''de facto'' "pursued a policy that combined official neutrality with growing support for Armenia," according to Svante Cornell. Cornell argues that Iran has "pursued policies in the conflict inclined towards Armenia."{{sfn|Cornell|1998|p=51}} However, Iran's tacit support for the Armenian side{{cite news |last1=Vatanka |first1=Alex |title=Tangle in the Caucasus: Iran and Israel Fight for Influence in Azerbaijan |url=https://www.foreignaffairs.com/articles/united-states/2013-01-15/tangle-caucasus#:~:text=Fearing%20Baku's%20intentions%20to%20fuel,in%20an%20inconclusive%20cease%2Dfire. |work=[[Foreign Affairs]] |date=15 January 2013 |quote=...Iran provided vital backing to Armenia in its war against Azerbaijan...}}{{cite journal |last1=Ehrmann |first1=Maya |last2=Kraus |first2=Josef |last3=Souleimanov |first3=Emil |title=The Iran-Israel-Azerbaijan Triangle: Implications on Regional Security |journal=Revista de Estudos Políticos |date=2013 |volume=4 |issue=7 |pages=215–228 |url=https://periodicos.uff.br/revista_estudos_politicos/article/view/40296 |publisher=[[Fluminense Federal University]]|doi=10.22409/rep.v4i7.40296 |quote="...Iran's support of Armenia in the Nagorno-Karabakh conflict..."}}{{cite news |last1=Herszenhorn |first1=David M. |title=Iran and Azerbaijan, Already Wary Neighbors, Find Even Less to Agree On |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2012/06/06/world/middleeast/iran-and-azerbaijan-wary-neighbors-find-less-to-agree-on.html |work=[[The New York Times]] |date=5 June 2012 |quote=...Iran's support of Armenia, Azerbaijan's western neighbor and sworn enemy, in the long-running war over the disputed territory of Nagorno-Karabakh."}} was limited to economic cooperation.{{cite book |last1=Rudolph |first1=Joseph Russell |title=Hot Spot: North America and Europe |date=2008 |publisher=[[ABC-CLIO]] |isbn=978-0313336218 |pages=[https://books.google.com/books?id=io6T82jEoK8C&dq=iran+support+armenia+karabakh&pg=PA185 185–186] |quote=Iran supports Armenia [...] To be sure, the assistance provided to Armenia and Azerbaijan respectively by these neighboring states has been limited to diplomatic support and occasional economic favors.}} [[Terhi Hakala]] noted in 1998 that "as a geopolitical counter-weight to Turkey, Iran has strongly supported Armenia, especially by alleviating the effects of the Turkish blockade."{{cite journal |last1=Hakala |first1=Terhi |author-link1=Terhi Hakala |title=The OSCE Minsk Process: A balance after five years |journal=[[Security and Human Rights|Helsinki Monitor]] |date=1998 |volume=9 |issue=1 |page=13 |url=https://brill.com/view/journals/hels/9/1/article-p5_1.xml?language=en |publisher=[[Netherlands Helsinki Committee]] |doi=10.1163/157181498X00015 |issn=0925-0972|url-access=subscription }} Cornell notes that during the war, Iran served as Armenia's "main purveyor of electricity and goods, and once the Armenian conquest of Karabakh had been completed, Iranian trucks began to supply most of the secessionist enclave's needs."{{cite book |last1=Cornell |first1=Svante E. |author-link1=Svante E. Cornell |title=Azerbaijan Since Independence |date=2015 |publisher= Routledge |isbn=978-1317476214 |pages=[https://books.google.com/books?id=TaZzCQAAQBAJ&dq=iran+economic+armenia+karabakh&pg=PA321 321–322]}} According to Bahruz Balayev, "Iran supported the territorial integrity of Azerbaijan and gave some humanitarian aid to the [Azerbaijani] refugees, but in the meantime widely cooperates with Armenia and even Karabakh Armenian authorities."{{cite book |last1=Balayev |first1=Bahruz |title=The Right to Self-determination in the South Caucasus: Nagorno Karabakh in Context |date=2013 |publisher=Rowman & Littlefield |isbn=978-0739178270 |page=[https://books.google.com/books?id=pDISeBcDMEcC&dq=iran+support+armenia+karabakh&pg=PA69 69] |chapter=Islamic Republic of Iran}} [[Brenda Shaffer]] wrote that "Iran's cooperation with Armenia and its tacit support in the conflict with Azerbaijan over Karabagh strengthened Yerevan's actual and perceived power and consequently may have lessened its sense of urgency to resolve the conflict."{{cite journal |last1=Shaffer |first1=Brenda |author-link1=Brenda Shaffer |title=It's not about ancient hatreds, it's about current policies: Islam and stability in the Caucasus |journal=Caucasian Regional Studies |date=31 October 2000 |volume=1–2 |issue=5 |url=https://www.belfercenter.org/publication/its-not-about-ancient-hatreds-its-about-current-policies-islam-and-stability-caucasus |publisher=[[Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs]]}} In 2013, [[Mohsen Rezaee]], who was commander of the [[Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps]] (IRGC) during the war, claimed that he "personally issued an order [...] for the Republic of Azerbaijan army to be equipped appropriately and for it to receive the necessary training." Rezaee added that "Many Iranians died in the Karabakh War. In addition to the wounded, who were transported to [Iran], many of the Iranian martyrs of the Karabakh War are buried in Baku."{{cite news |last1=Kucera |first1=Joshua |title=Iranian Official: We Helped Azerbaijan In Karabakh War |url=https://eurasianet.org/iranian-official-we-helped-azerbaijan-in-karabakh-war |agency=[[EurasiaNet]] |date=8 March 2013 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20200806082425/https://eurasianet.org/iranian-official-we-helped-azerbaijan-in-karabakh-war |archive-date=6 August 2020}} In 2011, [[Hassan Ameli]], a leading Iranian cleric, claimed that Iran provided Azerbaijan with arms and helped the [[Afghan mujahideen|Afghan mujaheddin]] move to Azerbaijan.{{cite news |title=Иран снабжал оружием Азербайджан и доставлял в Карабах бойцов из Афганистана: иранский аятолла |url=https://regnum.ru/news/polit/1402476.html |work=regnum.ru |agency=[[REGNUM News Agency]] |date=9 May 2011 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20190819233106/https://regnum.ru/news/polit/1402476.html |archive-date=19 August 2019 |language=ru}}{{cite news |title=Top Iran Cleric Says Afghan Rebels Fought for Azerbaijan |url=http://asbarez.com/95788/top-iran-cleric-says-afghan-rebels-fought-for-azerbaijan/ |work=[[Asbarez]] |date=10 May 2011 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20200806083119/http://asbarez.com/95788/top-iran-cleric-says-afghan-rebels-fought-for-azerbaijan/ |archive-date=6 August 2020}} The Iranian embassy in Armenia stated that they would not like unreliable information to affect friendly Armenian-Iranian relations: "We do not exclude the possibility that there are forces, which aim to create hindrances for our friendly relations."{{cite news |title=Iranian embassy in Yerevan avoids commenting on weapons supply to Baku |url=http://panarmenian.net/m/eng/news/69513 |agency=[[PanArmenian.Net]] |date=10 May 2011 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20200806083118/http://panarmenian.net/m/eng/news/69513 |archive-date=6 August 2020}} In October 2020, several protests erupted in Iranian cities, including the capital [[Tehran]] and [[Tabriz]], in support of Azerbaijan,{{cite web|title=Protests Erupt In Iran Backing Azerbaijan In Nagorno-Karabakh Conflict|url=https://www.rferl.org/a/protests-erupt-in-iran-backing-azerbaijan-in-nagorno-karabakh-conflict/30870217.html|date=2 October 2020|archive-date=9 October 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201009193448/https://www.rferl.org/a/protests-erupt-in-iran-backing-azerbaijan-in-nagorno-karabakh-conflict/30870217.html|url-status=live|work=Radio Free Europe/Radio Liberty}}{{cite news |title=Iran fears spillover from Nagorno-Karabakh |url=https://www.dw.com/en/iran-fears-spillover-from-nagorno-karabakh/a-55250556 |work=[[Deutsche Welle]] |date=12 October 2020}} with many [[Iranian Azerbaijanis]] chanting pro-Azerbaijan slogans and protesting Iran's alleged arms support to Armenia via the [[Nurduz|Nordooz]] border crossing.{{cite news |title=Iran on edge as Azeri minority backs Karabakh war |url=https://asiatimes.com/2020/10/iran-on-edge-as-azeri-minority-backs-karabakh-war/ |work=[[Asia Times]] |date=8 October 2020}} ====United States==== [[File:170216-D-PB383-024 (32809150501).jpg|thumb|[[Joseph Dunford]], chairman of the [[Joint Chiefs of Staff]], with Azerbaijani Minister of Defense [[Zakir Hasanov]] on 16 February 2017]] [[Thomas Ambrosio]] suggested in 2000 that the US "supported Azerbaijan's territorial integrity, but enacted policies that effectively supported Armenia's irredentist policies."{{cite book |last1=Ambrosio |first1=Thomas |author-link1=Thomas Ambrosio |title=Irredentism: Ethnic Conflict and International Politics |date=2000 |publisher=Greenwood Publishing Group |isbn=978-0275972608 |page=[https://books.google.com/books?id=0hLzXEO-fAQC&dq=United+States+supported+Azerbaijan%27s+territorial&pg=PA147 147]}} [[Sergo Mikoyan]] argued in 1998 that the US response to the conflict has been "inconsistent, pulled in different directions by the legislative and executive branches of power." Congress was under the influence of the Armenian lobby, while the executive branch (the White House and the State Department) pursued a pro-Azerbaijani policy, which "reflects Turkish influence and the interests of oil companies."{{cite journal |last1=Mikoyan |first1=Sergo |author-link1=Sergo Mikoyan |title=Russia, the US and regional conflict in Eurasia |journal=[[Survival (journal)|Survival]] |date=1998 |volume=40 |issue=3 |page=120 |doi=10.1080/00396338.1998.9688528 |publisher=[[International Institute for Strategic Studies]]}} [[Richard C. Longworth]] and Argam DerHartunian expressed similar views.{{cite journal |last1=Longworth |first1=Richard C. |author-link1=Richard C. Longworth |title=Boomtown Baku |journal=[[Bulletin of the Atomic Scientists]] |date=June 1998 |volume=54 |issue=3 |page=[https://books.google.com/books?id=ygsAAAAAMBAJ&dq=pro-azeri%20Karabakh&pg=PA37 37] |doi=10.1080/00963402.1998.11456843 |bibcode=1998BuAtS..54c..34L |quote=Washington has two foreign policies toward the region, one pro-Azeri, the other anti-Azeri. The pro-Azeri policy belongs to the administration, which listens to the oil companies. The anti-Azeri policy belongs to Congress, which listens to the Armenian lobby.}}{{cite journal |last1=DerHartunian |first1=Argam |title=Negotiation and Settlement in Nagorno-Karabak: Maintaining Territorial Integrity or Promoting Self-Determination |journal=Pepperdine Dispute Resolution Law Journal |date=2007 |volume=7 |issue=2 |page=301 |url=https://digitalcommons.pepperdine.edu/drlj/vol7/iss2/6/ |publisher=[[Pepperdine University]]}} [https://digitalcommons.pepperdine.edu/cgi/viewcontent.cgi?article=1094&context=drlj PDF] ([https://web.archive.org/web/20200825091254/https://digitalcommons.pepperdine.edu/cgi/viewcontent.cgi?article=1094&context=drlj archived])" "While the executive department favors Azerbaijan in the conflict, Congress has partially sided with Armenia because of the Armenian-American lobbying efforts." [[United States Congress|Congress]]'s pro-Armenian position was expressed in passing the [[Section 907]] of the [[Freedom Support Act]] in 1992, which banned any assistance to Azerbaijan. It was effectively amended by the Senate in 2001 and waived by President [[George W. Bush]] starting from 2002.{{cite web |title=Presidential Determination on Azerbaijan |url=https://georgewbush-whitehouse.archives.gov/news/releases/2002/01/20020128-20.html |website=georgewbush-whitehouse.archives.gov |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160922175429/http://georgewbush-whitehouse.archives.gov/news/releases/2002/01/20020128-20.html |archive-date=22 September 2016 |date=25 January 2002}} The US provides [[United States military aid|military aid]] to both countries. Between 2005 and 2016 Azerbaijan received $8.5 million for counternarcotics assistance and $11.5 million for counterterrorism aid. In the same period, Armenia received only $41,000 for counternarcotics assistance and none for counterterrorism aid. According to [[EurasiaNet]], "Much of the money for Azerbaijan has been targeted toward naval forces, to reduce the risk that it could be used against Armenia."{{cite news |last1=Kucera |first1=Joshua |title=Azerbaijan Has Advantage Over Armenia In U.S. Military Aid |url=https://eurasianet.org/azerbaijan-has-advantage-over-armenia-us-military-aid |work=[[EurasiaNet]] |date=17 May 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200715161149/https://eurasianet.org/azerbaijan-has-advantage-over-armenia-us-military-aid |archive-date=15 July 2020}} The [[First presidency of Donald Trump|Trump administration]] greatly increased the US military aid to Azerbaijan to around $100 million in fiscal years 2018–19, compared to less than $3 million in a year in FY 2016–17. The US aid is primarily "offered in the context of U.S. policy to increase pressure on Iran and focuses on Azerbaijan's Iranian border, but it also has implications for Armenia," according to Emil Sanamyan. In FY 2018, Armenia received $4.2 million in U.S. security assistance.{{cite web |last1=Sanamyan |first1=Emil |title=US Allocates $100 million in Security Aid to Azerbaijan in 2018–19 |url=https://armenian.usc.edu/us-allocates-100-million-in-security-aid-to-azerbaijan/ |website=armenian.usc.edu |publisher=[[USC Institute of Armenian Studies]] |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200714181348/https://armenian.usc.edu/us-allocates-100-million-in-security-aid-to-azerbaijan/ |archive-date=14 July 2020 |date=17 July 2019}} The US has also provided humanitarian aid to Artsakh (some $36 million between 1998 and 2010),{{cite web |title=Legislative History of U.S. Assistance to Nagorno Karabakh |url=https://anca.org/assets/pdf/misc/US_assistance_to_NKR.pdf |website=anca.org |publisher=[[Armenian National Committee of America]] |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200811144238/https://anca.org/assets/pdf/misc/US_assistance_to_NKR.pdf |archive-date=11 August 2020}} including for [[demining]].{{cite news |last1=Kucera |first1=Joshua |title=U.S. ends funding for Karabakh demining |url=https://eurasianet.org/us-ends-funding-for-karabakh-demining |agency=[[EurasiaNet]] |date=19 March 2020 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20200811143505/https://eurasianet.org/us-ends-funding-for-karabakh-demining |archive-date=11 August 2020}} The humanitarian aid has been criticized by Azerbaijan for legitimizing the "illegal regime in the occupied lands and damages the reputation of the US as a neutral mediator."{{cite news |last1=Mir – Ismail |first1=Alman |title=US-Azerbaijani Relations Cooling |url=https://jamestown.org/program/us-azerbaijani-relations-cooling/ |work=jamestown.org |agency=[[Jamestown Foundation]] |date=11 January 2010 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20200811143142/https://jamestown.org/program/us-azerbaijani-relations-cooling/ |archive-date=11 August 2020}} ===Arms suppliers=== In 1992, the OSCE "requested its participating states to impose an embargo on arms deliveries to forces engaged in combat in the Nagorno-Karabakh area." However, it is a "voluntary multilateral arms embargo, and a number of OSCE participating states have supplied arms to Armenia and Azerbaijan since 1992."{{cite web |title=OSCE arms embargo on Nagorno-Karabakh (Azerbaijan) |url=https://www.sipri.org/databases/embargoes/eu_arms_embargoes/azerbaijan |website=sipri.org |publisher=[[Stockholm International Peace Research Institute]] |archive-url=https://archive.today/20200807110215/https://www.sipri.org/databases/embargoes/eu_arms_embargoes/azerbaijan |archive-date=7 August 2020 |date=2 September 2018}} The [[United Nations Security Council Resolution 853|UN Security Council Resolution 853]], passed in July 1993, called on states to "refrain from the supply of any weapons and munitions which might lead to an intensification of the conflict or the continued occupation of territory." According to [[Stockholm International Peace Research Institute|SIPRI]], "since 2002, the UN Security Council has no longer listed that it is 'actively seized of the matter'. As such, since 2002, it is assumed that the non-mandatory UN embargo is no longer active."{{cite web |title=Non-mandatory UN arms embargo on Nagorno-Karabakh (Azerbaijan) |url=https://www.sipri.org/databases/embargoes/un_arms_embargoes/nagorno-karabakh-azerbaijan-non-mandatory/non-mandatory-un-arms-embargo-on-nagorno-karabakh-azerbaijan |website=sipri.org |publisher=[[Stockholm International Peace Research Institute]] |archive-url=https://archive.today/20200814115723/https://www.sipri.org/databases/embargoes/un_arms_embargoes/nagorno-karabakh-azerbaijan-non-mandatory/non-mandatory-un-arms-embargo-on-nagorno-karabakh-azerbaijan |archive-date=14 August 2020 |date=4 October 2012}} ====Armenia==== Russia has long been Armenia's primary arms supplier. Smaller suppliers include [[China]],{{cite news |last1=Harutyunyan |first1=Sargis |title=New Chinese Rockets 'Acquired By Armenia' |url=https://www.azatutyun.am/a/25080110.html |work=azatutyun.am |agency=[[RFE/RL]] |date=19 August 2013 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200724082733/https://www.azatutyun.am/a/25080110.html |archive-date=24 July 2020}}{{cite journal |last1=Rolland |first1=Nadège |title=China's Ambitions in Eastern Europe and the South Caucasus |journal=Notes de l'Ifri |date=December 2018 |issue=112 |page=8 |url=https://www.ifri.org/sites/default/files/atoms/files/rolland_china_eastern_europe_south_caucasus.pdf |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190107232905/https://www.ifri.org/sites/default/files/atoms/files/rolland_china_eastern_europe_south_caucasus.pdf |url-status=dead |archive-date=7 January 2019 |publisher=[[Institut français des relations internationales]] |quote=In 1999, China made its first arms sale in the Caucasus by supplying WM-80 rocket launchers to Armenia.}} [[India]],{{cite news |title=India pips Russia and Poland for $40m Armenia defence deal |url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/india/india-pips-russia-and-poland-for-40m-armenia-defence-deal/articleshow/74432148.cms |work=[[The Times of India]] |date=2 March 2020}}{{cite news |last1=Upadhyaya |first1=Shishir |title=India Wins Defense Deal With Armenia in Bid to Chasten Turkey |url=https://thediplomat.com/2020/03/india-wins-defense-deal-with-armenia-in-bid-to-chasten-turkey/ |work=[[The Diplomat (magazine)|The Diplomat]] |date=18 March 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200628223309/https://thediplomat.com/2020/03/india-wins-defense-deal-with-armenia-in-bid-to-chasten-turkey/ |archive-date=28 June 2020}} [[Ukraine]],{{cite news |last1=Kucera |first1=Joshua |title=Tajikistan Buying Guns; ====Azerbaijan==== According to SIPRI, Russia supplied 55% of Azerbaijan's weaponry in 2007–11,{{cite news |last1=Zolyan |first1=Mikayel |title=Karabakh, statements and arms deals |url=https://www.balcanicaucaso.org/eng/Areas/Nagorno-Karabakh/Karabakh-statements-and-arms-deals-139345 |work=balcanicaucaso.org |publisher=[[Osservatorio Balcani e Caucaso Transeuropa]] |date=19 |url=https://eurasianet.org/report-azerbaijan-gets-85-percent-of-its-weapons-from-russia |access-date=28 July 2020 |agency=[[EurasiaNet]] |date=18 March 2015 |title=USA and France dramatically increase major arms exports; Saudi Arabia is largest arms importer, says SIPRI |archive-date=14 August 2020 |date=9 March 2020}} [[Israel]] has become a major supplier,{{cite web|last1=Murinson |first1=Alexander |title=The Ties Between Israel and Azerbaijan |url=http://besacenter.org/wp-content/uploads/2014/10/MSPS110-web.pdf |website=Mideast Security and Policy Studies No. 110 |publisher=[[Begin–Sadat Center for Strategic Studies]] |date=October 2014 |quote=Israel supported the Azeri side in this conflict by supplying Stinger missiles to Azerbaijani troops during the war. |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141103080836/http://besacenter.org/wp-content/uploads/2014/10/MSPS110-web.pdf |archive-date=3 November 2014}} * {{cite news |last1=Kucera |first1=Joshua |title=Azerbaijan Makes Massive Israeli Weapons Purchase – But Not Because of Iran |url=https://eurasianet.org/azerbaijan-makes-massive-israeli-weapons-purchase-but-not-because-of-iran |work=[[EurasiaNet]] |date=27 February 2012 |quote=...these weapons are destined to be used not against Iran, but against Armenia...}} * {{cite news |last1=Cohen |first1=Gili |title=Azerbaijan's |quote=Azerbaijan has purchased nearly $5 billion-worth of defense equipment from Israel...}} accounting for 60% of Azerbaijan's arms imports in 2015–19. Azerbaijan's other suppliers include [[Turkey]], [[Belarus]],{{cite news |last1=Rahimov |first1=Rahim |title=Azerbaijan Shows off Polonez, LORA Missiles From Belarus, Israel |url=https://jamestown.org/program/azerbaijan-shows-off-polonez-lora-missiles-from-belarus-israel/ |access-date=14 June 2018 |work=jamestown.org |publisher=[[Jamestown Foundation]] |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200724100739/https://jamestown.org/program/azerbaijan-shows-off-polonez-lora-missiles-from-belarus-israel/ |archive-date=24 July 2020}} [[Canada]] (via Turkey),{{cite news|url=https://www.theglobeandmail.com/politics/article-canada-issued-permits-for-export-of-target-acquisition-gear-to-turkey/|title=Canada issued permits for export of target acquisition gear to Turkey in May despite arms embargo|author=Steven Chase|date=2 October 2020|newspaper=[[The Globe and Mail]]}}{{cite news|url=https://www.theglobeandmail.com/politics/article-canadian-made-targeting-gear-used-in-air-strikes-against-armenians/|title=Canadian equipment used in air strikes against Armenians|author=Steven Chase|date=30 October 2020|newspaper=[[The Globe and Mail]]|page=A1}} Ukraine,{{cite book|last=Azadian|first=Edmond Y.|title=History on the Move: Views, Interviews and Essays on Armenian Issues|year=1999|publisher=[[Wayne State University Press]]|isbn=978-0814329160|page=[https://books.google.com/books?id=KGciVUhpzXUC&pg=PA173 173]|quote=But as subsequent events evolved it became all too apparent that Ukraine has steadfastly stood behind Azerbaijan in the Nagorno-Karabagh conflict all along. ...it was reported from Stepanakert that Ukraine had shipped 40 tanks to Azerbaijan. |agency=[[Organized Crime and Corruption Reporting Project]] |date=18 September 2019 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20200807110113/https://www.occrp.org/en/daily/10703-czech-weapons-end-up-in-azerbaijan-despite-embargo |archive-date=7 August 2020}}{{cite news |last1=Dawkins October 2019 |archive-date=7 August 2020|quote=Weapons that Jaroslav Strnad and Excalibur sold to Israeli firm Elbit had immediately arrived in Azerbaijan...}} (denied by the Czech authorities).{{cite news |title=Milan Štěch: Czech Republic did not sell weapons to Azerbaijan (video) |url=https://a1plus.am/en/article/173028 |work=a1plus.am |agency=[[A1plus]] |date=4 October 2017 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20200807110609/https://a1plus.am/en/article/173028 |archive-date=7 August 2020}} ===Foreign fighters=== Several foreign groups fought on both sides in the intense period of fighting in 1992–94. According to [[Human Rights Watch]] (HRW), both sides used [[mercenaries]] during the war, namely "Russian, Ukrainian, and Belorussian mercenaries or rogue units of the Soviet/Russian Army have fought on both sides."{{sfn|HRW|1994|p=106}} ====Azerbaijan==== Azerbaijan made extensive use of mercenary pilots. According to a 1994 report from Human Rights Watch, "Most informed observers believe that mercenaries pilot most of Azerbaijan's air force."{{sfn|HRW|1994|p=110}} Several foreign groups fought on the Azerbaijani side: Chechen militants, Afghan mujahideen,{{cite news |last1=Sneider |first1=Daniel |title=Afghan Fighters Join Azeri-Armenian War |url=https://www.csmonitor.com/1993/1116/16071.html |work=[[The Christian Science Monitor]] |date=16 November 1993 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20200812102846/https://www.csmonitor.com/1993/1116/16071.html |archive-date=12 August 2020}} members of the [[Turkish nationalism|Turkish ultranationalist]] [[Grey Wolves (organization)|Grey Wolves]],{{harvnb|Chorbajian|Mutafian|Donabedian|1994|p=34}}: "Alpaslan Turkesh, founder of the Turkish fascist Grey Wolves, acknowledged that his followers were fighting in Karabagh with Azerbaijani forces, though it was reported in late 1992 that they had returned to Turkey." and the [[Ukrainian nationalism|Ukrainian nationalist]] and neo-fascist [[Ukrainian National Assembly – Ukrainian People's Self-Defence|UNA-UNSO]].In a 2010 interview, [[Mykola Karpyuk]], a leader of the UNA-UNSO said that "many Ukrainians", including members of the organization fought on the Azerbaijani side. {{cite news |last1=Baiyev |first1=Bakhram |title=В случае войны мы окажем Баку посильную помощь |url=http://www.vesti.az/news.php?id=53312 |work=vesti.az |date=17 September 2010 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100918061524/http://www.vesti.az/news.php?id=53312 |url-status=dead |archive-date=18 September 2010 |language=ru}} * {{cite news |title=Украинские националисты УНАО-УНСО признали, что воевали на стороне Азербайджана в Карабахе |url=https://www.panorama.am/ru/news/2010/09/17/mikola-karpyuk/1056213 |work=panorama.am |date=17 September 2010 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170517033224/http://www.panorama.am/ru/news/2010/09/17/mikola-karpyuk/1056213 |archive-date=17 May 2017 |language=ru}} The Chechen fighters in Karabakh were led by [[Shamil Basayev]], who later became [[Prime Minister of Ichkeria]] (Chechnya), and [[Salman Raduyev]].{{sfn|Taarnby|2008|p=9}} Basayev famously participated in the [[Battle of Shusha (1992)|battle of Shusha]] in 1992.{{sfn|Taarnby|2008|p=9}}{{sfn|De Waal|2003|p=179}} Saudi-born [[Ibn al-Khattab]] may have also joined them.{{cite news |last1=Khalilova |first1=Konul |title=Chechen Fighter's Death Reveals Conflicted Feelings in Azerbaijan |url=https://eurasianet.org/chechen-fighters-death-reveals-conflicted-feelings-in-azerbaijan |work=eurasianet.org |agency=[[EurasiaNet]] |date=14 May 2002 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20200819093922/https://eurasianet.org/chechen-fighters-death-reveals-conflicted-feelings-in-azerbaijan |archive-date=19 August 2020 |quote=Some say he joined the Chechen guerrillas fighting on Azerbaijan's side during the 1992–93 Nagorno-Karabakh war, though Ashurov and the Ministry of Defense's spokesman dismiss this idea.}} The Afghan mujahideen were mostly affiliated with the [[Hezb-e Islami Gulbuddin|Hezb-e Islami]], led by Afghan Prime Minister [[Gulbuddin Hekmatyar]].{{sfn|Taarnby|2008|p=6}}{{sfn|HRW|1994|p=81}} According to HRW, they were "clearly not motivated by religious or ideological reasons" and were, thus, mercenaries.{{sfn|HRW|1994|p=81}} The recruitment of Afghan mujahideen, reportedly handled by [[Special Purpose Police Unit|paramilitary police]] chief [[Rovshan Javadov]], was denied by Azerbaijani authorities.{{sfn|HRW|1994|p=81}}{{sfn|Taarnby|2008|pp=5–6}} They first arrived to Azerbaijan in fall 1993 and numbered anywhere between 1,500 and 2,500{{sfn|HRW|1994|p=81}} or 1,000 and 3,000.{{sfn|Taarnby|2008|p=7}} Armenia alleged that they were paid for by Saudi Arabia.{{sfn|Taarnby|2008|p=6}} Afghan mujahideen constituted the most considerable influx of foreign fighters during the war.{{sfn|Taarnby|2008|p=7}} Some 200 Grey Wolves were still present in the conflict zone as of September 1994 and were engaged in training Azerbaijani units.{{cite book |author1=[[Center for Strategic and International Studies]] |editor1-last=Brzezinski |editor1-first=Zbigniew |editor2-last=Sullivan |editor2-first=Paige |editor1-link=Zbigniew Brzezinski |title=Russia and the Commonwealth of Independent States: Documents, Data, and Analysis |date=1997 |publisher=[[M.E. Sharpe]] |isbn=978-1563246371 |page=[https://books.google.com/books?id=Vt5OLD3vp4UC&pg=PA616 616] |quote=It is also revealed that a new force of 200 armed members of the Grey Wolves organization has been dispatched from Turkey in preparation for a new Azeri offensive and to train units of the Azeri army.}} ====Artsakh and Armenia==== Some 85 Russian [[Kuban Cossack]]s and around 30 [[Ossetians|Ossetian]] volunteers fought on the Armenian side.{{cite news |title=Памятник-хачкар погибшим за освобождение Карабаха кубанским казакам открылся в НКР [Monument-khachkar to the Kuban Cossacks who died for the liberation of Karabakh opened in NKR] |url=https://newsarmenia.am/news/nagorno_karabakh/karabah-20110530-42463816/ |work=newsarmenia.am |agency=Novosti Armenia News Agency
